text:
an error of six
months. On the 11th October at 2-13 a.m., there is a square to
radical Mars and at 8-6 a.m. I find another square to radical
Mercury. The first gives the date 8th April 1951, and the
second gives 6th July following. 1t was during this period that
there appeared to be some improvement. On the 12th October
(representing 1952 September onwards), I find at birth time
the Moon in Capricorn 10 deg. 33'. But the worst period began
in September and it has come to a close by the last week of
December. The whole method seems to fail in accounting for
facts. I may be reminded of the M.C. which is 20 deg. 2' in
Virgo. But the M.C. was exact square to M.C. on lOth October
at 3-15p.m., or in the last week of October 1950. If this square

112 Rectification of Birth Time
is a decisive event, it must have occurred in the middle of
January 1950; and then I will have to accept the thirteenth
degree of Virgo as the M.C. This is roughly twenty-eighth
minutes earlier then the given time.
